core_payload.content:
# Reminder

## 1. Role
Reminder is a stronger follow-through system for N1Hub, where reminders act as context-aware commitment threads rather than timed pings.

## 2. Reminder Contract
The reminder layer knows when to resurface work, when to escalate, when to stay quiet, and how to fit the user’s actual rhythm instead of blindly replaying deadlines. It carries richer quiet-window sensitivity, better return paths, and clearer coordination with tracker, notification, and calendar layers.

## 3. Operational Consequence
Intentions stay alive at the right moment without turning the system into ambient pressure. Reminder quality becomes a core part of execution trust rather than an afterthought of scheduling.

## 4. Philosophical Alignment
Reminder supports To Dig Deep by protecting commitments without letting the second brain become adversarial.

neuro_concentrate.summary: Reminder defines the follow-through system that keeps commitments alive at the right moment without turning N1Hub into ambient pressure. It covers context-aware commitment threads, escalation logic, quiet-window handling, return paths, and coordination with tracker, notification, and calendar surfaces. The capsule matters because execution trust erodes when reminders are either noisy or absent. It keeps intentions resurfacing with the right timing and context so commitments stay actionable, humane, and rhythm-aware instead of behaving like blind deadline replays.

epistemic_ledger.rationale: Stripped future-meta phrasing so the capsule states present planning doctrine directly.
